questions.q: In later Hindu tradition, Varuna is best known as the lord of which element?
questions.explanation: Though a sky-god in the Vedas, Varuna came in later texts to be revered as the lord of the waters and oceans.

questions.q: Which cosmic and moral order does Varuna uphold and protect in the Vedas?
questions.explanation: In the Vedas, Varuna is the great guardian of Rita, the cosmic and moral order that governs all things.

questions.q: What weapon or instrument is Varuna famously said to carry to bind wrongdoers?
questions.explanation: Varuna is depicted binding sinners and wrongdoers with his noose, the pasha.

questions.q: Varuna belongs to which class of solar deities, the sons of the goddess Aditi?
questions.explanation: Varuna is counted among the Adityas, the deities born of the goddess Aditi.

questions.q: Over which of the four cardinal directions does Varuna preside as a guardian (Lokapala)?
questions.explanation: As a guardian of the directions, Varuna presides over the west.

questions.q: Which sea-creature serves as the mount (vahana) of Varuna in later imagery?
questions.explanation: Varuna is portrayed riding the Makara, a sea-creature that serves as his vahana.

questions.q: In which ancient collection of hymns is Varuna among the most exalted and frequently invoked deities?
questions.explanation: Varuna is one of the most exalted deities invoked in the hymns of the Rigveda.

questions.q: Varuna is said to watch over all human deeds with what remarkable feature?
questions.explanation: Varuna is described as the all-seeing god who observes every deed with a thousand eyes.

questions.q: As the keeper of cosmic order, Varuna is chiefly concerned with which aspect of human conduct?
options: Truth and the punishment of falsehood
questions.explanation: Varuna upholds truth and righteousness, binding and punishing those who break their word or commit falsehood.

questions.q: In the Vedas, Varuna is frequently paired and invoked alongside which other sovereign deity?
questions.explanation: Varuna is most often paired with Mitra, and the two are jointly invoked as the dual divinity Mitra-Varuna.

questions.q: The dual divinity in which Varuna is paired with Mitra is commonly known by what compound name?
questions.explanation: Varuna and Mitra together form the celebrated Vedic dual divinity Mitra-Varuna.

questions.q: Which goddess is named as the consort of Varuna in later Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Varunani is named as the consort of Varuna in later Puranic tradition.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, when Rama sits in protest at the seashore, which god finally appears to grant passage to Lanka?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Varuna, the lord of the ocean, appears before Rama and allows the sea to be bridged toward Lanka.

questions.q: In the Vedic worldview, Varuna's authority is closely tied to his association with which celestial expanse?
options: The encircling sky and cosmic waters
questions.explanation: In the Vedas Varuna is a sky-sovereign whose dominion encompasses the encircling heavens and cosmic waters.

questions.q: The class of deities to which Varuna belongs takes its name from which mother goddess?
questions.explanation: The Adityas, including Varuna, are named after their mother, the goddess Aditi.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the diseases of dropsy and edema were sometimes associated with the displeasure of which god, who binds with his noose?
questions.explanation: Varuna, who binds wrongdoers with his noose, was traditionally associated with afflictions such as dropsy.

questions.q: Which sage is described in tradition as having been born from a pot through the agency of Mitra and Varuna?
questions.explanation: The sage Vasishtha is traditionally said to have been born from a pot in connection with Mitra and Varuna.

questions.q: In the Puranas, Varuna is honored as the sovereign or king over which group of beings of the deep?
questions.explanation: As lord of the waters, Varuna is regarded as sovereign over the aquatic creatures and beings of the deep.

questions.q: Varuna's role as a Lokapala means he serves primarily as what?
options: A guardian of one of the directions
questions.explanation: As a Lokapala, Varuna is one of the guardian deities who protect the cardinal directions, presiding over the west.

questions.q: Over the course of the tradition, Varuna's status shifts from a supreme Vedic sovereign to which more specialized role?
options: Lord of waters and the western quarter
questions.explanation: Once a near-supreme Vedic deity, Varuna later becomes more specifically the lord of the waters and guardian of the west.

questions.q: According to tradition, which warrior of the Mahabharata received the bow Gandiva, which is associated with Varuna's gift to Agni?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata's burning of the Khandava forest, the bow Gandiva connected with Varuna comes into the hands of Arjuna.

questions.q: In the Rigveda, Varuna is frequently given which honorific title, used in its early sense of a mighty lord possessing wondrous power?
questions.explanation: In the Rigveda Varuna is repeatedly called an Asura, the term used in its early, honorific sense of a mighty lord wielding wondrous power (maya).

questions.q: In the Rigveda, Varuna's mysterious creative power and command over the cosmic order is denoted by which term?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda attributes to Varuna a profound maya, a wondrous power by which he establishes and sustains the cosmic order.

questions.q: During the churning of the ocean of milk, the goddess of wine and intoxication who emerged is named after Varuna; what is her name?
questions.explanation: Among the treasures of the churning of the ocean, the goddess Varuni, associated with wine, arose and bears Varuna's name.

questions.q: In the Rigveda, Varuna is often said to dwell in a golden-clad abode supported by what?
questions.explanation: Rigvedic hymns describe Varuna seated in a lofty mansion said to rest upon a thousand pillars.

questions.q: The famous Vedic hymns confessing sin and pleading for forgiveness from a binding god are addressed chiefly to which deity?
questions.explanation: Several of the most heartfelt Rigvedic hymns of confession and plea for mercy are directed to Varuna, who forgives transgression.

questions.q: In the tale of the Vedic seer Shunahshepa, who was bound to be sacrificed, deliverance is sought above all from which god?
questions.explanation: In the story of Shunahshepa, the youth bound for sacrifice appeals to Varuna and the gods for release.

questions.q: Varuna is sometimes invoked alongside Mitra and Aryaman as a triad of which kind of deities?
options: Sovereign Adityas of law and order
questions.explanation: Varuna, Mitra, and Aryaman form a sovereign triad of Adityas associated with law, contract, and moral order.

questions.q: In Vedic cosmology, Varuna is credited with regulating the heavens, the waters, and especially which natural cycle?
questions.explanation: Varuna is celebrated for governing the celestial and earthly waters, releasing the rains and directing the flow of rivers.

questions.q: Within the Vedic pantheon, which thunder-wielding deity gradually eclipsed Varuna in prominence over time?
questions.explanation: Over the course of the Vedic period, Indra, the warrior king of the gods, rose to eclipse the once-supreme Varuna.
